content: Date 2026-03-07. I need to make a clear decision for the Cairn mobile app: go React Native or go Flutter. I'm choosing React Native — familiarity wins over Flutter's performance story. I'm Sam, 31, backend/ML at a Paris logistics startup. We already have a TypeScript web codebase and two frontend devs comfortable with JS/TS; none of us are Flutter people. Key facts I keep in my head: shared validation/business logic with web matters for speed of iteration; we need an MVP in one delivery cycle (targeting 2026-09-30); our user base will include drivers on low-end Android phones, so perf is important but not the only constraint; hiring a dedicated mobile engineer earliest by Q1 2027 if we grow. Decision: React Native (bare, not Expo) to allow native modules for maps/scanner and to reuse JS logic. I'm saying 'familiarity wins' because ramp time for Flutter will cost weeks and neural nets + backend features we need are quicker to test if we reuse TS libs. I'm thinking Hermes, Fastlane for CI/CD, Sentry for crash/trace, and a small device lab of 15–20 physical devices for testing. Costs: initial dev runway — 1 FTE + 1 contractor for 3 months — roughly €30k–€45k external spend depending on contractor rates. Notes: Flutter's performance is attractive, but profiled RN with Hermes + careful optimization will be fine for 90% of our use cases. I want the decision recorded and a short plan of immediate next steps.
What's the single metric or milestone by 2026-06-07 that will validate this choice?

OK: metric and milestone. By 2026-06-07 I want a working RN skeleton app with auth, navigation, and one native module (barcode scanner) integrated and tested on at least 5 real devices. Success criteria: build passes CI, cold-start <1.5s on our mid-range test device, and crash-free users >98% in smoke testing. If we miss that, we re-evaluate a 4-week Flutter spike. Also: log this decision as 'React Native — chosen 2026-03-07' in our product repo.
What's the biggest technical risk you foresee in hitting that 2026-06-07 milestone?

Biggest risk: low-end Android performance and unpredictable native module bugs. Mitigations: 1) performance budget and profiling integrated from day one (Flipper + Hermes traces), 2) a 15–20 device lab including the cheapest phones we support, 3) isolate risky features as native modules with clear interfaces so we can ship JS UI while native team stabilizes scanner/maps. Operationally: I own onboarding the contractor, set up the monorepo (pnpm workspaces), and create the RN skeleton by 2026-03-14. Dev plan: sprint 1 (3 weeks) — skeleton + auth; sprint 2 (3 weeks) — navigation + scanner native module; sprint 3 (3 weeks) — polish + CI + device testing. Estimate external contractor cost in range €15k–€25k for the 3 sprints; internal costing covered by existing headcount.
Who will own the native bridge code and its maintenance after launch?

Short answer: me and the contractor for the initial phase; handoff to a full-time mobile hire in Q1 2027 for long-term maintenance. Details: I'll write the initial native-bridge contract doc (API, performance SLAs, tests) by 2026-03-10, contractor implements; everything goes into the monorepo with native test suites and a short onboarding doc. If we need to, we'll scope a small retainer with the contractor for the first 6 months post-launch (€2k/month) until we hire. Also: if a native blocker is encountered we can ship a webview fallback for non-critical flows to keep momentum.
Which single non-functional goal will you refuse to compromise on (e.g., startup time, memory, crash rate)?

I will not compromise on crash rate: target crash-free >98% for every release. Startup time and memory are important but negotiable in the short term; crashes break trust with drivers. Implementation: Sentry with release health, block merges if crash-free <98% in staging; mandatory end-to-end smoke tests on the device lab before release. Action items logged now: 1) decision recorded 2026-03-07, 2) repo skeleton by 2026-03-14, 3) contractor onboarded by 2026-03-17, 4) first milestone (skeleton + scanner) by 2026-06-07, 5) re-evaluate if milestone missed. That's the plan — commit to RN.
Final check: confirm you commit to React Native now and the immediate repo-skeleton milestone 2026-03-14?
